mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or predictive R-squared drops below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall signals supply chain bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, which compress corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO up, HOUST down) fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a directional accuracy above 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During an early tightening cycle, simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index trigger a repricing of risk premiums, discounting future cash flows more heavily and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral in over 50% of triggered tightening episodes, or the joint signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales directly reflects aggregate consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue guidance and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The sign of RSAFS monthly changes fails to predict the sign of 5-day SPX returns with accuracy exceeding 52% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production exceeding 2 percent over three months indicates severe supply-side disruption, which forces input cost inflation and reduces equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative following INDPRO contractions > 2%, or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signals a wage-price spiral risk, prompting market repricing of margin compression and inflation expectations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to produce a negative information coefficient over any 2-year rolling window, or forward 60-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, accelerating real GDP growth combined with rising housing starts confirms broad-based economic normalization, which lifts equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1/HOUST signal predicts negative 5-day returns in more than 40% of recovery episodes, or the predictive R-squared remains below 0.005 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.
